text.title: Secrets Management: Examples
text.summary: Vault Kubernetes auth setup, ESO ExternalSecret, SOPS encryption workflow, AWS Secrets Manager rotation.

example.note: Configure Vault Kubernetes auth method so pods authenticate using their ServiceAccount JWT.
example.code:
# Enable Kubernetes auth method vault auth enable kubernetes
# Configure it with the cluster's API server and CA vault write auth/kubernetes/config \ kubernetes_host="https://kubernetes.default.svc" \ kubernetes_ca_cert=@/var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/ca.crt \ token_reviewer_jwt=@/var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token
# Create a policy for the application vault policy write myapp-policy - <<EOF path "secret/data/production/myapp/*" { capabilities = ["read"] } path "database/creds/myapp-readonly" { capabilities = ["read"] } EOF
# Bind the policy to a Kubernetes ServiceAccount vault write auth/kubernetes/role/myapp \ bound_service_account_names=myapp \ bound_service_account_namespaces=production \ policies=myapp-policy \ ttl=1h

example.note: ESO ExternalSecret syncing a Vault secret to a Kubernetes Secret with field remapping.
example.code: # SecretStore (namespace-scoped, preferred over ClusterSecretStore) apiVersion: external-secrets.io/v1beta1 kind: SecretStore metadata: name: vault-backend namespace: production spec: provider: vault: server: https://vault.example.com:8200 path: secret version: v2 auth: kubernetes: mountPath: kubernetes role: myapp serviceAccountRef: name: myapp --- # ExternalSecret: syncs every hour, creates a K8s Secret apiVersion: external-secrets.io/v1beta1 kind: ExternalSecret metadata: name: myapp-db-creds namespace: production spec: refreshInterval: 1h secretStoreRef: name: vault-backend kind: SecretStore target: name: myapp-db-creds creationPolicy: Owner data: - secretKey: username remoteRef: key: production/myapp/database property: username - secretKey: password remoteRef: key: production/myapp/database property: password

example.note: Encrypt a secrets file with SOPS using AWS KMS, then decrypt in a GitOps pipeline.
example.code:
# Install SOPS curl -Lo sops https://github.com/getsops/sops/releases/download/v3.9.0/sops-v3.9.0.linux.amd64 chmod +x sops && sudo mv sops /usr/local/bin/
# .sops.yaml (checked into Git, no secrets) # creation_rules: # - path_regex: secrets/.*\.yaml # kms: arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789:key/xxxxxxxx
# Encrypt a secrets file (values are encrypted, keys remain readable) sops --encrypt secrets/production/myapp.yaml > secrets/production/myapp.enc.yaml
# Decrypt for local use sops --decrypt secrets/production/myapp.enc.yaml
# ArgoCD: install SOPS plugin or use helm-secrets # FluxCD: configure decryption provider in Kustomization # flux create kustomization myapp \ # --source=GitRepository/myapp \ # --path=./k8s \ # --decryption-provider=sops \ # --decryption-secret=sops-age

text.reference: ESO ExternalSecret manifest for Vault or AWS SM
Vault least-privilege policy for a K8s workload
